revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:عشيرة كيتاباتاكي]][[de:Kitabatake (Klan)]][[en:Kitabatake clan]][[es:Clan Kitabatake]][[fa:خاندان کیتاباتاکه]][[it:Clan Kitabatake]][[ja:北畠家]][[ko:기타바타케 가]][[pt:Clã Kitabatake]][[zh:北畠家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11403853]] Wikidata name: Kitabatake clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11403853]] Wikidata name: Kitabatake clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Samurai Clan}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | surname nihongo =北畠氏 | home province = [[:en:Ise Province|Ise]] | parent house = [[File:Sasa Rindo.svg|25px]] [[Minamoto clan]] |image=Sasa Rindō inverted.png|image caption=[[Mon (emblem)|Mon]] of Kitabatake clan}}
The '''Kitabatake clan''' was a clan that ruled south [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]] in Japan and had strong ties to the eastern provinces through Pacific sea routes.<ref name="BlumYasutomi2005">{{cite book|author1=Mark L. Blum|author2=Shin'ya Yasutomi|title=Rennyo and the Roots of Modern Japanese Buddhism|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=osIbxmUlCssC&pg=PA25|date=29 December 2005|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-535099-9|pages=25–}}</ref> Among its leaders included [[:en:Kitabatake Tomonori|Kitabatake Tomonori]].
==Clan heads== # Kitabatake Masaie (1215–1274, founder) # Kitabatake Morochika (1244–1315) # Kitabatake Moroshige (1270–1322) # [[Kitabatake Chikafusa]] (1293–1354) # [[:en:Kitabatake Akiyoshi|Kitabatake Akiyoshi]] (1326?–1383?) # Kitabatake Akiyasu (1361?–1414) # Kitabatake Mitsumasa (1382?–1429) # Kitabatake Noritomo (1423–1471) # Kitabatake Masasato (1449–1508) # Kitabatake Kichika (1468–1518) # Kitabatake Harutomo (1503–1563) # [[:en:Kitabatake Tomonori|Kitabatake Tomonori]] (1528–1576) # Kitabatake Tomofusa (1547–1580) # Kitabatake Tomotoyo ([[Oda Nobukatsu]]) (1558–1630)
==See also== *[[Kitabatake Shrine]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/kitabata.html Kamon World] {{in lang|ja}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Minamoto clan]]
}}
[[en:Kitabatake clan]]
[[Category:Pages without translation templates]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ar:عشيرة كيتاباتاكي]] [[de:Kitabatake (Klan)]] [[es:Clan Kitabatake]] [[fa:خاندان کیتاباتاکه]] [[it:Clan Kitabatake]] [[ja:北畠家]] [[ko:기타바타케 가]] [[pt:Clã Kitabatake]] [[zh:北畠家]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:عشيرة كيتاباتاكي]][[de:Kitabatake (Klan)]][[en:Kitabatake clan]][[es:Clan Kitabatake]][[fa:خاندان کیتاباتاکه]][[it:Clan Kitabatake]][[ja:北畠家]][[ko:기타바타케 가]][[pt:Clã Kitabatake]][[zh:北畠家]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:عشيرة كيتاباتاكي]][[de:Kitabatake (Klan)]][[en:Kitabatake clan]][[es:Clan Kitabatake]][[fa:خاندان کیتاباتاکه]][[it:Clan Kitabatake]][[ja:北畠家]][[ko:기타바타케 가]][[pt:Clã Kitabatake]][[zh:北畠家]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:عشيرة كيتاباتاكي]][[de:Kitabatake (Klan)]][[en:Kitabatake clan]][[es:Clan Kitabatake]][[fa:خاندان کیتاباتاکه]][[it:Clan Kitabatake]][[ja:北畠家]][[ko:기타바타케 가]][[pt:Clã Kitabatake]][[zh:北畠家]]
{{Short description|Samurai Clan}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | surname nihongo =北畠氏 | home province = {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise}} | parent house = [[File:Sasa Rindo.svg|25px]] [[Minamoto clan]] |image=Sasa Rindō inverted.png|image caption=[[Mon (emblem)|Mon]] of Kitabatake clan}}
The '''Kitabatake clan''' was a clan that ruled south {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}} in Japan and had strong ties to the eastern provinces through Pacific sea routes.<ref name="BlumYasutomi2005">{{cite book|author1=Mark L. Blum|author2=Shin'ya Yasutomi|title=Rennyo and the Roots of Modern Japanese Buddhism|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=osIbxmUlCssC&pg=PA25|date=29 December 2005|publisher=Oxford University Press|isbn=978-0-19-535099-9|pages=25–}}</ref> Among its leaders included {{ill|Kitabatake Tomonori|en|Kitabatake Tomonori|WD=Q10903777|lt=Kitabatake Tomonori}}.
==Clan heads== # Kitabatake Masaie (1215–1274, founder) # Kitabatake Morochika (1244–1315) # Kitabatake Moroshige (1270–1322) # [[Kitabatake Chikafusa]] (1293–1354) # {{ill|Kitabatake Akiyoshi|en|Kitabatake Akiyoshi|WD=Q11403872|lt=Kitabatake Akiyoshi}} (1326?–1383?) # Kitabatake Akiyasu (1361?–1414) # Kitabatake Mitsumasa (1382?–1429) # Kitabatake Noritomo (1423–1471) # Kitabatake Masasato (1449–1508) # Kitabatake Kichika (1468–1518) # Kitabatake Harutomo (1503–1563) # {{ill|Kitabatake Tomonori|en|Kitabatake Tomonori|WD=Q10903777|lt=Kitabatake Tomonori}} (1528–1576) # Kitabatake Tomofusa (1547–1580) # Kitabatake Tomotoyo ([[Oda Nobukatsu]]) (1558–1630)
==See also== *[[Kitabatake Shrine]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www2.harimaya.com/sengoku/html/kitabata.html Kamon World] {{in lang|ja}}
